name: Baked Tamales
recipeInstructions:
An early stage in the development of the deep-dish tamale pie, with the real turn-of-the-century flavor.
Recipe by Mrs. Bertha S. Morris, from "The Los Angeles Times Cookbook Number Three" (1908).
1. In a skillet, sauté beef until browned.
2. Add tomatoes, chili powder, garlic, salt, and black olives. Cook for 15 minutes.
3. Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish and line the bottom with half of the Corn Meal Dough.
4. Add meat mixture to the dish and cover with remaining Corn Meal Dough.
5. Bake at 325°F for 20 minutes or until browned.
Each serving contains about: 578 calories; 1,213 milligrams sodium; 132 milligrams cholesterol; 28 grams fat; 57 grams carbohydrates; 23 grams protein; 1.24 grams fiber.
CORN MEAL DOUGH:
1. Add corn meal and salt to water in a pot.
2. Bring mixture to boil and cook over medium heat until thick, about 5-10 minutes.
3. Add eggs and butter. Stir well.
